auth_notes: Legacy systems typically utilize Basic Authentication or a session-based approach where a login request is required to obtain a session cookie or token for subsequent requests.
rate_limits: Usually undefined or unmanaged; requires manual throttling using n8n 'Wait' nodes to prevent server overload.
gotchas: Heavy reliance on XML or SOAP payloads rather than modern JSON.
Non-standard error handling where 200 OK status codes may still contain error messages in the body.
Lack of native pagination, requiring complex loop logic in n8n.
client_must_provide: API Documentation (often in PDF or WSDL format)
Network access credentials (VPN, SSH Tunnel, or IP Whitelisting)
research_notes: Integration will require the HTTP Request node as the primary interface. Developers should be prepared to use the XML node for parsing responses if the system is SOAP-based. Custom error handling logic is essential to validate the content of successful HTTP responses.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will require the HTTP Request node as the primary interface. Developers should be prepared to use the XML node for parsing responses if the system is SOAP-based. Custom error handling logic is essential to validate the content of successful HTTP responses.
